meta.title: HELIX evolve: thread a new requirement through the artifact graph

narration: The classic example from the product vision: 'We want OAuth login.' One sentence. The agent invokes the evolve skill against existing artifacts.

prompt: /helix-evolve Add OAuth login alongside the existing API-key auth

assistant: I'll walk the artifact graph in authority order and find what this change touches.
Impact analysis — three feature specs, two solution designs, the security architecture, one ADR conflicts. Plan, ordered by authority:

output:
step 1  · revise security-architecture.md (token lifecycle, refresh, revocation)
  step 2  · supersede ADR-002 (API-key-only) with ADR-007 (dual-auth)
  step 3  · update FEAT-AUTH spec; add user stories US-OAUTH-{login,refresh,revoke}
  step 4  · revise SD-AUTH and SD-SESSION designs
  step 5  · update TP-AUTH test plan to cover both flows
  step 6  · file beads bead-OAUTH-{01..05} against the updated specs

assistant: Each downstream change traces back to step 1. Nothing in implementation lands until the security architecture lands.

narration: One sentence in. Six ordered steps out, anchored in the authority hierarchy. No vibe-coded auth. No drift.
